title: Buttery Flaky Kenyan Sweetcorn Pilaf
description: An energizing diabetic-friendly dish featuring sweetcorn and infused with the flavors of a tandoor oven, perfect for breakfast or as a snack.

ingredients.name: 1 cup cooked sweetcorn
ingredients.notes: Use low glycemic corn if available.

ingredients.name: 2 tbsp unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Opt for a softer variety for easier spreading.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup whole wheat breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: Make sure they are not stale and absorb well with the sweetcorn.

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: Citrus can help balance sweetness but reduce to half if using fresh.

ingredients.name: Pinch of salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust for taste, aim for low sodium when diabetic-friendly options are used.

ingredients.name: Freshly grated nutmeg
ingredients.notes: For a hint of spice and depth.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup whole milk (or dairy-free alternative)
ingredients.notes: A touch of creaminess to the dish.

ingredients.name: 1 tbsp honey or agave syrup (optional, reduce for diabetic-friendly version)
ingredients.notes: Optional sweetener that can be adjusted as needed.

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine cooked sweetcorn, whole wheat breadcrumbs, lemon juice, salt, nutmeg, and honey or agave syrup.
Add unsalted butter, breaking it into small pieces until well mixed with the corn mixture. The key is to distribute the butter evenly throughout.
Pour the mixture onto a greased baking dish (non-stick spray works great) and spread it out evenly.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown on top and bubbly underneath.
Allow the pilaf to cool slightly before serving. You can serve immediately, or keep warm in a low oven.
Enjoy your sweet yet savory, buttery flaky Kenyan Sweetcorn Pilaf as a snack or light meal.
